content: Existing Self-Refine frameworks, which rely on single-perspective iterative self-feedback to refine outputs without extra training, can be enhanced by integrating the multiple inconsistent solving perspectives central to LLM self-reflection mechanisms to break self-reinforcing feedback blind spots. This creates a hybrid 'multi-perspective self-refine' approach that retains Self-Refine's key advantage of no additional supervised training while improving output quality by surfacing unrecognized flaws a single self-feedback loop would miss.
